params.text:
Welcome to Stoma Care

Welcome to Stoma Care. Many of the people you support live with a stoma, and the everyday care around it has a direct effect on their comfort, confidence and dignity. This course helps you understand what a stoma is, the main types you may come across, and how clean, careful, person-centred support keeps the skin healthy and helps you notice problems early.

  • Understand: what a stoma is and why people have one.
  • Support: everyday care with dignity, privacy and kindness.
  • Report: when and how to raise concerns with the stoma nurse.

Awareness only — know your limits

Supporting a person with their stoma may be a delegated task. This course raises your awareness only. You should only carry out hands-on stoma care — such as emptying or changing a pouch — if you have had task-specific training, been assessed as competent, and the task is set out in the person's care plan. Wherever a person can manage their own stoma, support them to stay independent.

This course does not authorise you or anyone else to carry out stoma care without training, assessed competence and the care plan, and it does not replace the assessment of a registered stoma care nurse. If anything looks or feels abnormal, you stop and refer it to the stoma nurse or a registered nurse. Throughout, remember that your most important skills are noticing changes and reporting them promptly.
